results.title: Schneider Electric and Switch Expand Partnership with $1.9 Billion Supply Capacity Agreement to Power AI Factories
results.text:
Schneider Electric and Switch Expand Partnership with $1.9 Billion Supply Capacity Agreement to Power AI Factories Accessibility Statement Skip Navigation

- Largest data center cooling project in North America marks first deployment of Schneider Electric's Uniflair™ chillers in the U.S.
- Added capacity further positions Switch as a leading data center campus designer, builder and operator
- Agreement represents Schneider Electric's largest cooling services engagement to date

LAS VEGAS, Nov. 19, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Schneider Electric, a global energy technology leader, and Switch, a premier provider of AI, cloud and enterprise data centers, today announced a two-phase supply capacity agreement (SCA) totaling $1.9 billion in sales. The milestone deal includes prefabricated power modules and the first North American deployment of chillers. The announcement was unveiled at Schneider Electric's Innovation Summit North America in Las Vegas, convening more than 2,500 business leaders and market innovators to accelerate practical solutions for a more resilient, affordable and intelligent energy future.

Schneider Electric and Switch have evolved their longstanding partnership to support the growing AI and hyperscale computing demand of AI factories. Hyperscalers are growing rapidly to meet surging AI demand, which is projected to account for over 35% of global data center workloads by 2030, driving a 160% increase in data center power demand. By integrating advanced cooling and p

results.title: Schneider Electric's $2.3 Billion Worth of AI Power and Cooling Deals Sends Message to Data Center Sector | Data Center Frontier
results.text:
Schneider Electric's $2.3 Billion Worth of AI Power and Cooling Deals Sends Message to Data Center Sector | Data Center Frontier

AI / Machine Learning

# Schneider Electric's $2.3 Billion in AI Power and Cooling Deals Sends Message to Data Center Sector

Twin supply-capacity agreements by Schneider Electric with Switch and Digital Realty show how AI data centers are starting to lock up power and thermal infrastructure in much the same way as hyperscalers once locked up cloud chips.

11 min read

Add Us On Google

## Key Highlights

- Schneider Electric's $2.3 billion in U.S. data center contracts reflect a focus on multi-year supply capacity agreements, ensuring long-term supply of power and cooling systems for AI infrastructure.
- These agreements signal industry confidence in durable AI demand, pre-allocating resources similar to chip supply chains, and raising the bar for vendors to offer integrated 'chip-to-grid' solutions.
- Switch's partnership with Schneider positions the company at the forefront of exascale AI campuses, showcasing high-density, modular cooling and power solutions tailored for future AI workloads.
- Digital Realty's agreement with Schneider secures critical power gear for its expansive AI-focused data centers, standardizing equipment and reducing deployment timelines amid supply constraints.
- The convergence of power and cooling into strategic platforms indicates a shift where these resources are now key, constrained assets, integral to AI data cente

Schneider Electric announced on Wednesday that it had signed two separate supply agreements with Switch and Digital Realty, worth $1.9 billion and $373 million (€1.64 billion and €322.16 million) respectively, to meet demand in AI and data centers in the United States.

These two partnership extensions with US data center providers were announced at an innovation summit organized by Schneider Electric in Las Vegas, USA.

The two-phase agreement with Switch includes prefabricated power modules and the first deployment of Schneider Electric's Uniflair chillers in the United States, according to a statement from the French group.

# Schneider Electric and Switch pen $1.9bn cooling deal

Largest cooling contract ever in the American market, firms claim

November 19, 2025 By Matthew Gooding

Have your say

https://www.addtoany.com/share

Switch Data Centers and Schneider Electric have agreed an expanded $1.9 billion contract that they say is North America’s largest-ever data center cooling deal.

The supply capacity agreement (SCA) between the two firms will see Schneider supply prefabricated power modules for Switch’s data centers, as well as its Uniflair chillers. It will be the first time the chillers have been deployed in the US.

https://media.datacenterdynamics.com/media/images/The_Core_Switch.original.png

results.title: Schneider Electric and Digital Realty Announce $373M Supply Capacity Agreement to Meet Rising Data Center Demand
results.text:
Schneider Electric and Digital Realty Announce $373M Supply Capacity Agreement to Meet Rising Data Center Demand | Nasdaq

Skip to main content

- Longstanding relationship continues to accelerate energy technology and critical infrastructure capacity in the U.S.
- Agreement helps to bring capacity online faster to meet surging demand driven by AI

LAS VEGAS, Nov. 19, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Schneider Electric, a global energy technology leader, today announced that Digital Realty, (NYSE: DLR) the leading global provider of carrier-neutral data center, colocation, and interconnection solutions, has signed a $373 million Supply Capacity Agreement (SCA) for Uninterruptible Power Supply (UPS), Low Voltage Switchgear (LVS), and Pre-Fabricated Skids. The announcement was unveiled at Schneider Electric's Innovation Summit North America in Las Vegas, convening more than 2,500 business leaders and market innovators to accelerate practical solutions for a more resilient, affordable and intelligent energy future.

Building on years of close collaboration, Schneider Electric and Digital Realty have evolved their longstanding partnership to support rising capacity demands and deliver greater supply chain resilience. The strategic shift to an SCA model provides guaranteed capacity, economies of scale, and a dedicated LVS production line, while preserving the flexibility needed for a dynamic, multi-vendor environment to mitigate risk.

"As demand for digital infrastructure continues to accele

### Contract Opportunity Analysis

The Department of Defense, through the 184 Kansas Air National Guard, is seeking a small-business contractor to support the NOS weapon system relocation data center infrastructure effort at McConnell AFB in Kansas. The work covers engineering, design, procurement, and installation of a complete data center solution, including 38 racks or cabinets, aisle containment, 76 power distribution units, and an environmental monitoring system. The project is tied to a phased facility effort and must meet future SCIF accreditation-related constraints, including no RF-transmitting capabilities; the monitoring system must be on the DoDIN Approved Products List with Authority to Connect at submission, and the solicitation does not include low-voltage data cabling or fiber optics. Performance is scheduled for 180 days from award with a firm on-site installation window of July 8 to July 21, 2026, and quotes are due electronically by April 9, 2026 at 9:00 AM CST, with no award until

results.title: Data Center - Panduit
results.text:
Data Center - Panduit

# Data Center Infrastructure Solutions

Delivering Efficient, Reliable, and Scalable End-to-End Solutions

### Panduit offers a comprehensive portfolio of end-to-end solutions designed for diverse environments, including Hybrid IT, Hyperscale, Cloud, On-Premises, Colocation, and Edge.

### End-to-End Infrastructure Integration

Panduit is your single source for complete data center infrastructure power, fiber, cooling, cabinets, pathways, cable cleats, grounding and bonding, and beyond. Instead of managing multiple vendors, you get everything under one roof, backed by decades of expertise and global support. This one-stop approach simplifies procurement, accelerates deployment, and ensures every component integrates flawlessly, giving you confidence that your data center is built for performance, reliability, and future growth.

### Driving Digital Transformation

Your data center is the engine driving digital transformation. Whether you're managing an on-premises facility, a colocation site, hyperscale, or edge deployments, you need an infrastructure that is scalable, agile, and efficient to meet the demands of ever-evolving environments.

### Critical Power and Fiber Connectivity

When fiber and power work together, your infrastructure becomes stronger, smarter, and ready for the future. Panduit empowers modern data centers to handle AI-driven workloads, edge expansion, and sustainability demands through ultra-low-loss fiber connectivity to intelligen

Home | MSDM Data Centers

# Getting the job done

Precision. Performance. Predictability.

MSDM Data Centers delivers the complete CSA Fit-Out scope, from structure to finish, with full accountability. We manage our scope of work and control risk at every stage, staying closely connected with our clients and maintaining the momentum that keeps your project on schedule. This is how we deliver consistent results on some of Europe’s most demanding projects. With MSDM, clients gain certainty in quality, planning, EHS and execution; we help you get the job done. We meet — or even accelerate — delivery timelines while maintaining an outstanding safety record: zero lost time incidents over the last 5+ years.

## A single streamlined solution

Our experienced teams manage the complete internal fit-out of Data Centers efficiently, safely and with full integration. Whether you require selected packages or a full CSA Fit-Out, MSDM is your single point of contact and delivery. Products, installation, project management, EHS, quality management, compliance and document control are all fully integrated into our delivery. That’s the advantage of working with the Data Center specialist.

results.title: About us - Dutch PDU manufacturer for data centres
results.text:
About us - Dutch PDU manufacturer for data centres 

# Developed and built in the Netherlands. For rack-level energy control in data centres.

Schleifenbauer develops and manufactures PDU solutions for data centres, focused on delivering energy insight and control at rack level. We combine hardware and software into one scalable platform that provides real-time visibility, without complex licensing models or standard product limitations.

For data centres seeking control over energy, it starts with Schleifenbauer.

Free customisation

Made inThe Netherlands

One-stop Shopfor all PDUs

## Innovation and engineering excellence

All development takes place in-house with our experienced engineering team. This enables us to create flexible and forward-looking solutions, such as modular and hot-swappable communication modules and our complimentary EnerTree Data Center Energy Management Software. By continuously innovating together with leading data centre operators, we deliver PDUs that not only meet today’s requirements, but are ready for the future of digital infrastructure.

## Sustainability at the core

Sustainability and responsibility are integral to the way we operate. Our EcoVadis sustainability certification confirms our commitment to ethics, working conditions, responsible sourcing and environmentally conscious materials.

Development and production take place in the Netherlands, in collaboration with local and European suppliers. By keeping engineering and production in

### Custom Dimensions & Configurations

We offer complete flexibility in cabinet design with customizable dimensions, layouts, and internal configurations. Whether you need specific rack heights, cooling zones, or cable management setups, our OEM data center solutions are tailored to meet unique project requirements across industries.

### Full Process In-house Production

From sheet metal fabrication to final assembly and quality inspection, every stage of manufacturing is handled in-house. Our vertically integrated process ensures consistency, faster lead times, and better control over cost and quality for all OEM data center equipment.

### International Packing & Quick Shipping

To support global deployment, we provide professional export packing, container optimization, and quick-shipping services. Your customized data center products can be delivered directly to end users or project sites worldwide, saving time and logistics costs.
